teams.name: Brooklyn Nets
teams.editorialNote: Loaded rookie allocation — the most cards of any NBA team in this product (10). Egor Dëmin, Nolan Traore, and Drake Powell all debut as Nets rookies. If you're betting on a rebuild, the cards are stacked for it.

teams.name: Dallas Mavericks
teams.editorialNote: The Flagg show. Dallas landed Cooper Flagg as the #1 pick and his paper /1 and Chrome SuperFractor /1 are effectively the faces of this entire product. Only 5 total cards for the team, but one of them is the card of the set.

teams.name: Memphis Grizzlies
teams.editorialNote: Zach Edey's Timeless Touch auto is arguably the most accessible NBA star auto in the product (1:72 Hobby). Cedric Coward Chrome Autos give Memphis two auto paths, which more than most NBA teams can say this year.

teams.name: New Orleans Pelicans
teams.editorialNote: Jeremiah Fears + Derik Queen = two rookies in one class. Derik Queen has auto access; Fears, infamously, does not. Another Panini-exclusive fingerprint.

teams.name: Philadelphia 76ers
teams.editorialNote: The VJ Edgecombe paradox. Philly's #3 pick has Base, Chrome Variation, Rookie Red, and inserts — but zero autographs. If you want VJ on card, Bowman delivers. If you want him signed, it's probably a Panini-exclusive situation.

teams.name: San Antonio Spurs
teams.editorialNote: Wemby gets his running mate. Dylan Harper + Carter Bryant rookies alongside the Victor Wembanyama Timeless Touch auto makes San Antonio the franchise-story team of this release. Future on paper.

teams.name: Utah Jazz
teams.editorialNote: Ace Bailey lands here after a draft-day that raised eyebrows across the hobby. Utah fans finally have a high-upside rookie to collect, and the Bailey Chrome SuperFractor /1 becomes one of the set's key parallels.

teams.name: Washington Wizards
teams.editorialNote: Tre Johnson III is the name but the product pattern repeats: base and inserts yes, autos no. Washington gets 9 total cards, second-highest after Brooklyn, but the auto cabinet is empty for the top name.

teams.name: BYU
teams.editorialNote: AJ Dybantsa + Richie Saunders. BYU's Dybantsa is the projected #1 2026 pick, and his 1st Bowman Chrome Prospect Auto is arguably the holy grail of the NCAA side of this set.

teams.name: Duke
teams.editorialNote: The Boozer paradox. Duke was supposed to be the big brand story — Cameron and Cayden Boozer both played there this year as top-10 2026 prospects — but both are completely absent from the checklist and autographs. What Duke DOES have: Emilee Skinner (W) as a headline, plus Derek Dixon, Sebastian Wilkins, and Caleb Foster on the men's side.

teams.name: Kansas
teams.editorialNote: Darryn Peterson leads a 3-player Kansas contingent (plus Bryson Tiller and Kohl Rosario) and lands the 2026 top-3 projection slot after Dybantsa. If Peterson goes #1 instead of Dybantsa, his 1st Bowman auto becomes the grail.

teams.name: UConn
teams.editorialNote: Sarah Strong headlines the women's side — a freshman phenom in UConn blue. Three more WCBB prospects (Serah Williams, Kelis Fisher) plus men's names Silas Demary Jr. and Tarris Reed Jr. give UConn the deepest school representation in the NCAA prospect pool.

editorialTeamsToWatch.headline: Flagg or nothing
editorialTeamsToWatch.reasoning: One card, but it's THE card of the set. Cooper Flagg's Chrome SuperFractor /1 is arguably the most important single card in basketball this year.

editorialTeamsToWatch.headline: Wemby gets his running mate
editorialTeamsToWatch.reasoning: Harper + Carter Bryant rookies joining the Wembanyama era is the franchise-building story of the year. Multiple auto chases.

editorialTeamsToWatch.headline: Rookie pileup
editorialTeamsToWatch.reasoning: Most cards of any NBA team (10). Dëmin, Traore, Powell all rookie 1st Bowmans. If the rebuild works, this roster looks prescient.

editorialTeamsToWatch.headline: Dybantsa's only address
editorialTeamsToWatch.reasoning: The projected #1 2026 pick has ONE college home and ONE 1st Bowman set. Compounding scarcity on scarcity.

editorialTeamsToWatch.headline: Watkins headquarters
editorialTeamsToWatch.reasoning: USC has Juju Watkins AND Kara Dunn on the women's side — two auto-eligible WCBB chases at one school.

editorialTeamsToWatch.headline: Sarah Strong's UConn
editorialTeamsToWatch.reasoning: UConn has the deepest NCAA prospect representation in this product (5 players), headlined by Sarah Strong's 1st Bowman Chrome Prospect Auto.

ncaaEditorial.overview: The NCAA side of 2025-26 Bowman Basketball is where long-term upside lives. 53 programs represented across men's and women's basketball, with the 2026 NBA Draft class projected as one of the strongest in years. The men's checklist leads with AJ Dybantsa (BYU), Darryn Peterson (Kansas), Koa Peat (Arizona), Caleb Wilson (UNC), and Nate Ament (Tennessee). The structural bet: if even half of these prospects hit, this release becomes the foundational 1st Bowman product for a generation.
ncaaEditorial.projectedDraftClass: Top 2026 NBA Draft projections represented here: AJ Dybantsa (BYU), Darryn Peterson (Kansas), Caleb Wilson (UNC), Nate Ament (Tennessee), Mikel Brown Jr. (Louisville), Koa Peat (Arizona), Kiyan Anthony (Syracuse — Carmelo's son), Jasper Johnson (Kentucky). Notable absences: Cameron Boozer, Cayden Boozer, Boogie Fland, Karter Knox — four top-tier names missing from the product despite being marketed in pre-release materials.
ncaaEditorial.womensSection: WCBB gets serious representation for the first time in a Bowman NBA product. 30 women's players in Paper Prospects, 30 in Chrome Prospects — a 30/70 women-to-men split. Headliners: Juju Watkins (USC), Madison Booker (Texas), Sarah Strong (UConn), Sienna Betts (UCLA), Raegan Beers (Oklahoma). The Hannah Hidalgo absence (Notre Dame) is the most notable WCBB omission.
